Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Associated Insulation Of California
Off Calendar Apr-29-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Harry M. Dorman, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, Proceedings reported by: Mitch Lyon , CSR#6840.

Patrick R. Campbell, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, CARROLL CLINE and RUEHL CLINE.
Order Granting waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing on Application for Default Prove-up, on file.
No appearance by defaulted defendant(s) ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA.
Default of Defendant(s) has been duly entered.
After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant(s) as follows:

IT IS ORDERED, ADJUDGED AND DECREED that the plaintiff CARROLL CLINE and Plaintiff RUEHL CLINE shall recover damages from defendant ASSOCIAED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA in the following amounts: As to Plaintiff CARROLL CLINE, $530,022.00 in Economic Damages, and $500,000.00 in Non-Economic Damages, and, as to Plaintiff RUEHL CLINE, $300,000.00 in Non-Economic Damages, for a combined total amount of $1,330,022.00.
The judgment shall take effect immediately.
The Clerk is ordered to enter the judgment.
Judgment signed in open court.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Boss Manufacturing Companys Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-21-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Todd M. Thacker of JACKSON, JENKINS & RENSTROM, LLP (415-982-3600), appearing for defendant / moving party Boss Manufacturing Company
Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Carroll Cline and Ruehl Cline

RULING:
DEFENDANT BOSS MANUFACTURING COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Boss Manufacturing Company's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to causes of action 1, 2 and 4 (negligence, strict liability and loss of consortium) and off calendar as to punitive damages. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Mr. Cline's deposition testimony and declaration and Mr. Ay's declaration create a triable issue whether Mr. Cline was exposed to asbestos-containing gloves attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs have dismissed their claim for punitive damages. The Court sustained defendant's objections to Carroll Cline's testimony regarding the gloves' asbestos content. The Court further denied defendant's all other objections. Details can be found in Reporter's notes. Order signed after the hearing. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Cathy Meyer of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #11596 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Albay Construction Companys Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgement, Or In The Alternatice Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-15-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jasun C. Molinelli of ARCHER NORRIS (925-930-6600), appearing for defendant / moving party Albay Construction Company
Chris Hersom of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Carroll & Ruehl Cline

RULING:
DEFENDANT ALBAY CONSTRUCTION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGEMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Albay Construction Company's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' strict liability and false representation causes of action is off calendar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Cline was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Cline's deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant owed a duty to Mr. Cline. By failing to demonstrate that plaintiffs submitted factually devoid responses to comprehensive contention discovery designed to elicit all evidence and information plaintiffs have to support their contention that defendant owed a duty to Mr. Cline, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiffs represent that they have dismissed their strict liability and false representation causes of action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Maria L. Bracken of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #11741 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (Amended) Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-14-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Nicole E. Gage of TUCKER ELLIS LLP (415-617-2105), appearing for defendant / moving party Spirax Sarco, Inc.
Chris Hersom of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Carroll Cline & Ruehl Cline

RULING:
DEFENDANT SPIRAX SARCO, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (AMENDED) OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Spirax Sarco, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Cline was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's separate statement failed to demonstrate that it propounded comprehensive contention discovery designed to elicit the evidence plaintiffs have to support their claims against it and that plaintiffs submitted factually devoid responses thereto. Further, Mr. Cline's deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because it demonstrates that he worked with internal components original to Sarco equipment. Mr. Radle's declaration is insufficient to sustain defendant's burden of negating an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Cathy Meyer of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #11596 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
